what_it_does: Defines a standalone HTML preview card for a surface color palette, showing five swatches with names, hex values, usage notes, and a light foreground dot on the dark swatch.
observations: Includes dsCard metadata for group, name, subtitle, and viewport.
Links to _card.css and uses CSS custom properties for fonts, radii, borders, and foreground colors.
Renders five surface entries: canvas, surface, muted, sunken, and dark.
States that surfaces are warm, excludes cool greys, and avoids pure white as the page background.
Uses inline background colors for each swatch chip.
standouts: The canvas token is marked with a star in the visible label.
Pure white is assigned to cards on canvas rather than page background.
The dark swatch includes an inverse light ink dot.
conventions: Uses a compact card-preview HTML structure with local styles in the head.
Uses a CSS grid row for evenly spaced swatches.
Combines design-token variables with literal hex color values.
Keeps each swatch as repeated markup containing chip, name, hex, and description.
concerns: The star marker next to canvas is not explained within the file.
Swatch color values are hard-coded inline rather than referenced through named CSS variables.
notes: A compact design-system handoff card documenting the intended warm surface layers and their usage roles.
questions: What does the star next to canvas indicate?
Are these hex values meant to become named CSS tokens elsewhere?
purpose_vs_actual: The title and dsCard metadata state a surface palette preview, and the file renders a five-swatch surface palette with usage labels.
apparent_themes: avoidance of cool greys and pure-white page canvas
intention_signals: It appears intended to communicate the surface color scale and where each layer should be used in interface composition.
novelty: The file explicitly separates pure white card surfaces from the warmer page canvas and visualizes inverse foreground on the dark surface.
contribution: Contributes a warm five-layer surface palette with role labels for page backgrounds, cards, panels, dropzones, placeholders, and dark overlays.
summary_for_embedding: HTML design-system preview card showing five warm surface color swatches with names, hex codes, usage descriptions, and card metadata.
intention_for_embedding: Created to document and visually communicate the surface palette roles for a ConceptV design-system handoff.
